# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a React application. Install Node.js, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, and serve it with a lightweight HTTP server. Keep the Dockerfile simple and easy to understand for our team.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14-alpine

# Set the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json to the container
COPY package*.json ./

# Install app dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy the rest of your application code to the container
COPY . .

# Build the production bundle
RUN npm run build

# Use an Nginx image for serving static files
FROM nginx:alpine

# Copy the built React app from the first stage into the Nginx root directory
COPY --from=0 /app/build /usr/share/nginx/html

# Expose the default HTTP port
EXPOSE 80

# Start Nginx server
CMD ["nginx", "-g", "daemon off;"]
